devnet_3_status.highlight: 5-client interop achieved: Zeam, Ream, Lantern, Ethlambda, leanSpec
DevNet-3 merged to main in Zeam; ready for long-running testnet
Checkpoint sync issues resolved across clients; further stability testing needed

devnet_4_progress.highlight: DevNet-4 spec PR up for review; recursive aggregation via leanMultisig
Blocked on LeanSig ZK-friendly encoding implementation for recursive aggregation
ZK-friendly encoding not yet implemented; timeline uncertain due to workload

devnet_5_design.highlight: DevNet-5 proposal: sign both block and proposer attestation using recursive aggregation
Security concern: current proofs are succinct but not zero-knowledge
Alternative: aggregate signatures with different messages per party using Snarks
Consensus: use separate keys for proposer/attester; simplest secure approach

infrastructure.highlight: Subnet count hardcoded as 1; needs config-based approach for multi-subnet testing
Plan to scale validators beyond minimal counts for realistic testing
DevNet recovered from 500-slot stall to 2,500 slots via checkpoint sync

organizational.highlight: Kamil stepping down as Quadrivium CEO; joining Ethereum Foundation
Fort Mode event April (analogous to Beam Day); focus on consensus research

action_items.action: Review DevNet-4 spec PR and provide feedback
Move subnet count from hardcoded to chain config
Spec DevNet-5 with separate proposer/attester keys approach

decisions.decision: DevNet-5 will use separate keys for block proposing and attesting (not ZK approach)
Short-term: use even/odd XMSS indices; long-term: dual key system with independent lifetimes
Do not assume zero-knowledge properties for consensus layer design

targets.target: End of week - DevNet-4 spec completion target
This week - Gene team wrapping up DevNet-2 work
